finding.summary: Spectrum tilts low-end-heavy for a clean electric guitar: 100 Hz at -18 dB sits 6 dB above 1 kHz (-24 dB) and 12 dB above 4 kHz (-30 dB). Dynamics (crest 12 dB, LRA 8 LU) and headroom (true peak -5.5 dB) are healthy, so no dynamics or gain issue is evident. No safe single-track move can be proposed because no FX chain is present on this track and its parent bus (Guitar Bus) processing is unmeasured.
finding.probable_cause: Likely a tonal characteristic from pickup type, playing style, or low-end shaping applied on the unmeasured Guitar Bus parent. The capture alone cannot distinguish pickup character from downstream bus EQ.
finding.confidence_reason: track.parent_track is "Guitar Bus" whose processing is not in this payload, so this capture cannot establish the track's bus context; confidence capped at low per routing rule. fx_chain is empty, so any in-FX move is unsupported.

evidence_refs.description: 100 Hz band at -18.0 dB, the loudest of the reported bands
1 kHz band at -24.0 dB, 6 dB below the 100 Hz band
4 kHz band at -30.0 dB, 12 dB below the 100 Hz band
Track feeds parent bus "Guitar Bus" whose processing is not in this payload
Empty FX chain on this track; no EQ/compressor visible to attribute the tilt to
12.0 dB crest factor indicates healthy, non-compressed dynamics
-5.5 dB true peak leaves comfortable headroom

proposal.reason: With no FX on the track and the Guitar Bus parent processing unmeasured, no verifiable single-track move is supported. Flag the low-end tilt for review on the parent bus instead.
